posts.author: garg.saurabh1975
posts.text: My view on recent quarterly update from Gargi Store count went up ( 126 stores at end of FY26 & 94 in Q1FY26 → 135 by 30 June 2026 ) - That’s meaningfully negative same-store sales growth on the mature cohort, once new-store contribution is stripped out. Q1FY27 press release offers a new framing that was not present in prior communication : “demand in Q1 and Q2 is typically softer due to seasonal factors.” Further in Q4FY26 concall, management reiterated confidence and did not signal a soft Q1. On comparing them to light jewellery players like caratlane and bluestone (though not a like-to-like…

posts.author: bibhor
posts.text: Hi All Jewellery companies are enjoying growth due rise in gold prices , volume growth is negative In case of Gargi , it’s mainly volume driven growth as items are sold on MRP basis .

posts.author: Priyank_Jain
posts.text: If we look at last 3 years Revenue of the Company, Q1 has been 13-18% of full year revenue Hence even if we assume Q1 FY27 is 15% at 30 Cr, Full year comes at 200cr which is about 33% growth from FY26 All Quarters will never be same, its quite an overreaction in an illiquid stock PS: Invested & Biased

posts.author: newrb
posts.text: I agree with what you said. One cannot expect a straight line growth. To me, there seems be two challenges market is reacting to - i) Q1 update indicates a growth in H2. Last year Q2 was quite good at 44Cr topline. Based on Q1 update, it seems right now that Q2 would be again a bad quarter Y-o-Y. ii) Q4 concall was on May 6th. With nearly 50% of Q1 gone, management tone did not indicate any slow down. I am waiting for price to stabilize a bit to add some more. Disc - holdign. No reco.

posts.author: Marathondreams
posts.text: My guess is its one quarter dip which market over reacted to. It would be naive to think 35% CAGR would be a straight line growth with each quarter growing 30%+. There would be hickups in any growing company. Now the challenge for the management is how quickly they adapt to this slower growth scenario and maybe make few plan B decisions on marketing and pricing ( remember its very high margin business so they can afford to lower prices on selective items to stimulate growth) Disclosure - Continue to add more on each price drop. This is not a buy/sell recommendation and I am not SEBI authorised…

posts.author: newrb
posts.text: Titan mentioned about buyer growth of early double digits. So what you mentioned makes sense. However, the expectation set by PNGS Gargi was 35% CAGR for the next few years. I guess this is the reason for stock price tanking. disc - invested with a small position

posts.author: vijaykumarmahto
posts.text: Revenue growth of other jewellery companies in Q1: Titan Jewellery: 40% Kalyan Jewellers: 38% Senco Gold: 60% At first glance Gargi’s Q1 growth of 11.6 percent appears comparatively weak. But that’s not the case. The large part of the growth of Tanisq, Kalyan and Senco was due to inflation in gold prices/inventory gains. Their actual growth could be somewhere between 7-12 percent. Unlike Titan,Kalyan or Senco, PNGS Gargi sells everyday use fashion jewellery, Sterling silver jewellery, 9KT gold jewellery, 14KT diamond jewellery, Gifting articles and therefore only a small part of its business b…

posts.author: P2018
posts.text: Will have to wait for management to give some more color. Other jewellery companies are more occassion based + gold driven (maybe). Management did flag Q3 and Q4 are better periods.

posts.author: Anuj2
posts.text: Isn’t this growth way too slow compared to other jewellery companies or am I reading it incorrectly ?

posts.author: P2018
posts.text: https://www.bseindia.com/stockinfo/AnnPdfOpen.aspx?Pname=d402554c-cf7d-48a7-ae23-e23dc4fc167f.pdf Grew by 11% given that it’s a slow quarter they will have to do a lot of heavy lifting in the balance quarters
